title: Entity-Level Validation
breadcrumbs: Validation at Field, Key, Occurrence, or Entity Level
text: Entity-Level Validation Validation of an entity can be initiated by the validate/e ProcScript statement or as part of the component validation. An entity represents a collection of occurrences, so the conditions for entity validation are: The data of one or more fields in an occurrence of the entity has been modified, and One or more occurrences of the entity have not yet been validated successfully. Validation Process The validation process for an entity is as follows: Uniface issues the validate/o ProcScript statement for each occurrence of the entity specified with validate/e . Uniface checks whether the number of occurrences is within the range of the specified minimum and maximums for the entity. Declarative Constraints at Entity Level The following declarative constraints exist at entity level: If compound primary or candidate keys are defined, all fields in the key must contain data (by default, incomplete keys fail validation). When a compound primary or candidate key is validated, Uniface first checks if the key is complete. A complete key means that all fields belonging to that key contain data, except for fields that have a syntax definition of LEN(0-0). If the key is not complete, Uniface sets $error to 2206 and activates the entity-level error trigger. The validation process fails, and the validateKey trigger (or any other trigger) is not activated. The total number of occurrences in the entity must be between the minimum and maximum specified entity properties. By default, this constraint is not defined. When an entity is validated, Uniface checks whether the number of occurrences is within the range of the minimum and maximum specified. If this validation check fails, Uniface sets $error , depending on the violated definition, and activates the entity-level error trigger. The function $error is set to: 0102 if the number of occurrences is less than the minimum 0103 if the number of occurrences is greater than the maximum Note: Occurrences created with ProcScript are validated before declarative validation for the entity is performed. Related Links Validate Determines whether the valerr data processing attribute is added for the selected entity node in the DTD tree. This attribute holds the occurrence or field validation error (depending on whether the selected item is an entity or field). Valid values are True or False (default). $error Returns the Uniface message number for the error. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
